behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_OFFSUP::look' (examine) handler for an office-supplies item. When the player inspects the object the process runs and emits a short descriptive bark.
The handler queries the item's frame/variant via Item::getFrame(this) (stored in a temporary local 'local1') and selects a short name based on that frame value.

Specific mappings observed: frames 0x00–0x03 -> "quill pen"; 0x04–0x05 -> "ink bottle"; 0x06–0x07 -> "seal"; 0x08–0x09 -> "parchment"; 0x0A -> "balance scale"; all other frames fall back to a generic "office supplies" bark.
Each branch emits a Bark (player-facing short description) and then the script suspends/ends the U8P_OFFSUP::look response.
notable_control_flow: U8P_OFFSUP::look calls Item::getFrame(this) and stores the result in local1, then branches on local1's value/range to choose which Bark to emit.
Every branch issues a Bark followed by a suspend; there is a default branch that returns the generic "office supplies" text for unspecified frames.
No global flags are read or written by this script; behavior is local to the item instance.
open_questions: Are the frame ranges exhaustive for all in-game variants of this item or do additional frames exist that should map to other labels? (confidence: medium)
Are the bark strings raw text or keys into a localization system in the engine? (confidence: low)
Does 'suspend' always terminate the U8P_OFFSUP::look process here or can subsequent engine behaviors occur after suspend for this object? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_OFFSUP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short, player-facing description for an office-supplies item variant when the player examines/looks at it by mapping the item's frame to a concise name and emitting a Bark.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_OFFSUP::look/examine action for the item (process type 0x0208).
Runs when the player inspects the object in the world (player 'U8P_OFFSUP::look' action).

function_map.name: U8P_OFFSUP::look
function_map.role: U8P_OFFSUP::look/examine handler that selects and emits a short description (Bark) based on the item's frame

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: engine/item API call used to obtain the item's frame/variant index; input: this (the current item instance)

external_calls.purpose: Return the item's frame/index so the script can determine which short description (Bark) to show for this item instance.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the 'U8P_OFFSUP::look' (examine) handler for an office-supplies item. When the player inspects the object the process runs and emits a short descriptive bark.
